questions.question: Representation of the statement 'All cats are mammals' using predicate logic is: (a) P(C,m) (b) M(C) (c) P(C,m) ∧ M(C) (d) C(m)
A rule in the form 'If P then Q' can be represented using predicate logic as: (a) P → Q (b) P ∧ Q (c) ¬P ∨ Q (d) P ∧ ¬Q
The statement 'John likes reading and playing cricket' can be represented using predicate logic as: (a) L(J) ∧ P(J,C) (b) L(J) ∨ P(J,C) (c) ¬L(J) ∧ ¬P(J,C) (d) ¬L(J) ∨ ¬P(J,C)
